Now we have a filthy body that has soخليه قبل التحليه تجدها في القران كثيرا ليس كمثله شيء وهو السميع البصير اللهing all first exped ‏and devoid of any incorrect belief then the correct belief comes into the heart for everything you know then learn this is how it work however if a person tries to mi that which is correct and that which is incorrect together in the same heart it will begin الطهور شطر الايمان والحمد لله تملا الميزان ان الحمد لله وسبحان الله والحمد لله تملان او تملا ما بين السماوات والارض سبحان الله الحمد لله والصلاه نور والصدقه برهان والصبر ضياء والقران حجه لك اوك قران كل الناس يغدو فبائع نفسه فمعتقها ‏into servitation byl we nar will in upcall to takeer والله اعلم وصلى الله وسلم على نبينا محمد وعلى اله واصحابه اجمعين والحمد لله رب العالمين وجزاكم الله خيرا.

summary: This lecture discusses a hadith from Riyadul Saliheen by Imam An-Nawawi, narrated by Abu Malik Al-Harith ibn Asim Al-Ashari. The central hadith states "Purification is half of faith." The speaker explains this hadith by breaking down the concept of faith into two fundamental components: negation (negating false beliefs and deities) and affirmation (affirming the worship of Allah alone). The speaker emphasizes the Islamic principle of "At-Tahliyah before At-Tahliyah" (purification before adornment), meaning one must first remove impurities and incorrect beliefs before adorning oneself with correct beliefs and practices. The lecture also mentions related hadith teachings about the virtues of various actions such as praise of Allah, prayer, charity, patience, and the Quran.
suggested_title: Purification is Half of Faith: Understanding Negation and Affirmation in Islamic Belief
